input.background: The oat bran illustrative example calculated a 95% confidence interval for μ that had a margin of error of 0.2753 mmol/L.
input.question: How large a study is needed to achieve a margin of error 0.2 with 95% confidence? We will use the sample standard deviation from the study as our estimate of σ.

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量
output.answer: n=(z_{1-\frac{\alpha}{2}} \frac{\sigma}{m})^2=(1.96 \frac{0.4335}{0.2})^2=18.50. Round this up to the next integer, so n=19. Because this number is less than 30, apply adjustment factor f=(df+3) /(df+1)=(18+3) /(18+1)=1.105 to the final result. Therefore, use n=1.105 \times 19=21.

margin_of_error_current.description: The margin of error for the current study's 95% confidence interval.

margin_of_error_target.description: The target margin of error for the new study's 95% confidence interval.

confidence_level.description: The confidence level used to calculate the confidence interval.

sample_standard_deviation.value: σ (to be estimated from the current study)
sample_standard_deviation.description: The sample standard deviation, used as an estimate of the population standard deviation.

sample_size.description: The required sample size to achieve the target margin of error with 95% confidence.
